name: San Francisco Zoo
summary_en: The San Francisco Zoo's civic record is one of planning and oversight. A 1998 Planning Commission motion certifies the final EIR for the Zoological Gardens Master Plan, noting it would demolish the Aviary, Lion House and Pachyderm House in a potential zoo historic district. A 1995 Public Works infrastructure master plan sets out water and utility improvements. The Budget Analyst's January 2000 performance audit, reviewed by a 2003 select committee, identifies about $4.53 million that could be reallocated under Zoological Society management. Records under the historic name Fleishhacker Zoo, from 1929 and 1965, describe its founding and growth. The zoo is operated by the Recreation and Park Department, whose FY2025 budget line totals about $250M.
